sessions.activity: Watch 'React Tutorial for Beginners' by Programming with Mosh (Chapters: Introduction, Setting Up, Components)
Start 'React Tutorial Full Course - Beginner to Pro' by SuperSimpleDev (Sections: Introduction, What is React?, JSX, Setting up a React project with Vite)
Read 'Learning React' by Alex Banks (Chapters: Introduction to React, Setting Up a Project, JSX)
Practice: Create a new React project using Vite. Build a simple 'Hello, World!' component and a 'Welcome Message' component.
Watch 'React JS Tutorial for Beginners (2025)' by Tech Jashwanth (Sections: Learn Components, JSX, Vite & Virtual DOM Quick)

days.project: Create a simple React application that displays a 'Hello, React!' heading and a custom 'Greeting' component showing your name.
days.notes: Day 1 focused on understanding what React is, how to set up a development environment (Vite), and core concepts like JSX and functional components. Practice is key for JSX syntax.

sessions.activity: Continue 'React Tutorial Full Course - Beginner to Pro' by SuperSimpleDev (Sections: Props, State - the useState Hook, Event Handling)
Read 'Learning React' by Alex Banks (Chapters: Props for Data Flow, Managing State with Hooks)
Practice: Build an interactive 'Counter' component with increment/decrement buttons and a 'Reset' button. Understand how state updates re-render components.
Watch 'React JS - React Tutorial for Beginners' by Programming with Mosh (Sections: Components, Props, State, Handling Events)

days.project: Develop a 'Text Changer' app where a button click changes the displayed text content. Implement two-way data flow using props and state.
days.notes: Day 2 introduced the essential concepts of Props for passing data down to components and State (using the `useState` hook) for managing data within components. Event handling was also covered.

sessions.activity: Continue 'React Tutorial Full Course - Beginner to Pro' by SuperSimpleDev (Sections: Conditional Rendering, Rendering Lists, Introduction to the useEffect Hook)
Read 'Learning React' by Alex Banks (Chapters: Conditional Rendering, Rendering Lists, Understanding `useEffect` Basics)
Practice: Create a 'Todo List' application. Implement adding new items, marking items as complete (conditional styling), and deleting items. Use `map()` for list rendering.
Explore basic `useEffect` examples (e.g., logging state changes, simple title updates) to understand its purpose in managing side effects.

days.project: Build a 'Show/Hide Element' component. Use a button to toggle the visibility of a paragraph or image. Extend the Todo List with a filter to show active/completed items.
days.notes: Day 3 covered displaying content conditionally, rendering dynamic lists of data, and a basic introduction to the `useEffect` hook for handling side effects after renders. The Todo List project is crucial for reinforcing these concepts.

sessions.activity: Continue 'React Tutorial Full Course - Beginner to Pro' by SuperSimpleDev (Sections: Controlled Components for Forms, Lifting State Up, Component Composition)
Read 'Learning React' by Alex Banks (Chapters: Handling Forms in React, Component Composition and Reusability)
Practice: Create a 'Simple Registration Form' with input fields for Name, Email, and Password. Handle form submission and display the input data. Make sure it's a controlled component.

Explore 'React and React Native' by Adam Boduch for broader context on component patterns and forms, if 'Learning React' feels insufficient. Experiment with `useEffect` cleanup functions (conceptually).

books: React and React Native by Adam Boduch (https://openlibrary.org/works/OL19545984W)
days.project: Build a 'Feedback Form' with a text area and a submit button. Display the submitted feedback below the form. Implement basic input validation (e.g., ensure text area is not empty).
days.notes: Day 4 focused on handling user input through forms using controlled components. Understanding component composition for building complex UIs from smaller parts is also key. `useEffect` was explored for more practical scenarios.

sessions.activity: Review key sections from 'React Tutorial Full Course - Beginner to Pro' by SuperSimpleDev on topics you found challenging. Revisit `useState`, `useEffect`, props, and state flow.

Brainstorm ideas for a mini-application. Consult 'React Projects' by Roy Derks for inspiration on project structure and features relevant to a beginner. (e.g., a simple product listing, a calculator, a basic weather app mockup).
Work on the chosen mini-application. Focus on applying all learned concepts: components, props, state, event handling, conditional rendering, lists, and forms.

Self-reflection: Document challenges encountered and solutions found. Plan next steps for continued React learning. (Optional: Watch 'React Tutorial in Hindi' by CodeWithHarry if language is preferred).

days.project: Build a 'Product Listing' page: Display a list of products (name, description, price, image URL - use dummy data). Allow users to add products to a 'shopping cart' (simple state management).
days.notes: Day 5 is dedicated to consolidating knowledge by building a practical mini-application. This project will help integrate all the core concepts learned throughout the week into a cohesive application. Reviewing concepts is crucial for long-term retention.
